pattern: A greedy left-to-right sweep with a difference-array trick tracks how many flips currently cover each index so each zero is fixed exactly once in linear time.
hints: Process positions from left to right; the leftmost remaining zero forces a flip that must start exactly there.
You only need to know the parity of how many flips so far cover the current index, not which flips they were.

Maintain a running flip count and an array marking where each window's effect ends; if the current value (adjusted by active flips) is zero, start a new window here, and if a window would run past the end, the task is impossible.

edge_inputs.description: single element already one needs no flips
window of two can never fix this configuration
overlapping windows each fix one trailing zero
window equals length and all zeros so one flip suffices
window equals length but a one in the middle blocks success

stress.description: large random binary array with a mid-size window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: answer may be -1 sometimes; both solution and oracle must agree
